title: Incursa.Quic Public API Surface Promotion Work Item
summary: Promote the connection- and listener-facing Incursa.Quic consumer facade, refine its lifecycle semantics from Microsoft test evidence, and hide the helper-layer implementation seams.
planned_changes:
- Add the public connection, stream, listener, options, error, and abort/direction types named in the slice.
- Promote the first server-side listener entry surface (`ListenAsync`, `AcceptConnectionAsync`) and the narrow server connection-options callback.
- Correct `QuicStreamType` to the approved direction-only public model.
- Keep the packet, frame, TLS, handshake, runtime, recovery, congestion, stream-identity, and lifecycle helper surfaces internal.
- Reconcile the public API analyzer files with the approved smaller public boundary.
- Preserve the client-connect and fuller TLS-auth work for the next slice and avoid broadening the public contract into middleware.
- Encode the close/dispose, error projection, read-side, listener-accept, cancellation, and option-shape semantics reflected by the Microsoft reference tests and the existing runtime/stream/listener-host seams.
out_of_scope: Adding client connection establishment in this pass.
Adding the full client-auth contract in this pass.
Adding new transport features beyond the approved facade.
verification_plan: Use API-diff and reflection-based surface checks, plus compile-time access tests, to prove the approved public types are visible and the listed seam types are not. Add focused consumer-behavior tests for `QuicStreamType` shape, close/dispose/error projection, read-side terminal behavior, listener setup validation, listener disposal semantics, accept cancellation, and the connection/stream/listener facade mapping to `QuicConnectionRuntime`, `QuicConnectionStreamState`, and `QuicListenerHost`. Keep the proof focused on surface shape rather than wire behavior; this slice does not require client connect, full TLS-auth, fuzzing, or benchmarks.
completion_notes: This item now includes the first server-side public entry surface. The internal transport engine stays intact, and the client-connect/TLS-auth follow-on remains open.

supplemental_sections.heading: Reference Behavioral Evidence
supplemental_sections.content:
- `C:\src\dotnet\runtime\src\libraries\System.Net.Quic\tests\FunctionalTests\QuicListenerTests.cs`
- `C:\src\dotnet\runtime\src\libraries\System.Net.Quic\tests\FunctionalTests\QuicConnectionTests.cs`
- `C:\src\dotnet\runtime\src\libraries\System.Net.Quic\tests\FunctionalTests\QuicStreamTests.cs`
- `C:\src\dotnet\runtime\src\libraries\System.Net.Quic\tests\FunctionalTests\QuicStreamConnectedStreamConformanceTests.cs`
- `C:\src\dotnet\runtime\src\libraries\System.Net.Quic\tests\FunctionalTests\MsQuicCipherSuitesPolicyTests.cs`
- `C:\src\dotnet\runtime\src\libraries\System.Net.Quic\tests\FunctionalTests\MsQuicTests.cs`

These reference tests are the behavioral evidence for the initial public surface shape and the lifecycle semantics that are now implemented and proven in the target repo.
